record.name: Category
record.statement:
A category $\mathcal{C}$ consists of:

A collection $\text{Ob}(\mathcal{C})$ of objects.

For each pair of objects $A, B$, a set $\text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, B)$ of morphisms from $A$ to $B$.

For each object $A$, an identity morphism $\text{id}_A \in \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, A)$.

For each triple $A, B, C$, a composition map
$$
\circ : \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(B, C) \times \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, B) \to \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, C)
$$

subject to:

**Associativity.** $h \circ (g \circ f) = (h \circ g) \circ f$ for all composable $f, g, h$.

**Unit laws.** $f \circ \text{id}_A = f = \text{id}_B \circ f$ for all $f : A \to B$.

record.name: Uniqueness of identity
record.statement: The identity morphism at each object is unique. If $e \in \text{Hom}(A, A)$ satisfies $f \circ e = f$ for all $f$ with domain $A$ and $e \circ g = g$ for all $g$ with codomain $A$, then $e = \text{id}_A$.
record.proof: $e = e \circ \text{id}_A = \text{id}_A$, where the first equality uses the right-unit property of $\text{id}_A$ and the second uses the left-unit property of $e$. $\square$

record.name: Set and Grp
record.statement: The category $\textbf{Set}$ has sets as objects, functions as morphisms, and function composition as composition. The category $\textbf{Grp}$ has groups as objects and group homomorphisms as morphisms.

record.name: Initial object
record.statement: An object $I \in \mathcal{C}$ is *initial* if for every object $A$ there exists a unique morphism $I \to A$.

record.name: Uniqueness of initial objects
record.statement: If $I$ and $I'$ are both initial in $\mathcal{C}$, then they are uniquely isomorphic: there exists a unique isomorphism $I \xrightarrow{\sim} I'$.
record.proof: By initiality of $I$ there is a unique $f : I \to I'$; by initiality of $I'$ there is a unique $g : I' \to I$. Then $g \circ f : I \to I$ must equal $\text{id}_I$ by uniqueness, and similarly $f \circ g = \text{id}_{I'}$. $\square$

record.name: Functor
record.statement:
Let $\mathcal{C}$ and $\mathcal{D}$ be categories. A *functor* $F : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}$ consists of:
1. A map $F : \text{Ob}(\mathcal{C}) \to \text{Ob}(\mathcal{D})$ on objects.
2. For each pair $A, B \in \text{Ob}(\mathcal{C})$, a map $F_{A,B} : \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, B) \to \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{D}}(F(A), F(B))$ on morphisms.

subject to:

- **Identity preservation.** $F(\text{id}_A) = \text{id}_{F(A)}$ for all $A$.
- **Composition preservation.** $F(g \circ f) = F(g) \circ F(f)$ for all composable $f, g$.

record.name: Faithful functor
record.statement: A functor $F : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}$ is *faithful* if each map $F_{A,B} : \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, B) \to \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{D}}(F(A), F(B))$ is injective.

record.name: Full functor
record.statement: A functor $F$ is *full* if each $F_{A,B}$ is surjective. A functor that is both full and faithful is *fully faithful*.

record.name: Composition of functors
record.statement: If $F : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}$ and $G : \mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{E}$ are functors, then $G \circ F : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{E}$ defined by $(G \circ F)(A) = G(F(A))$ and $(G \circ F)(f) = G(F(f))$ is a functor.
record.proof: Identity: $(G \circ F)(\text{id}_A) = G(F(\text{id}_A)) = G(\text{id}_{F(A)}) = \text{id}_{G(F(A))}$. Composition: $(G \circ F)(g \circ f) = G(F(g \circ f)) = G(F(g) \circ F(f)) = G(F(g)) \circ G(F(f))$. $\square$

record.name: Composition of faithful functors
record.statement: The composition of faithful functors is faithful.
record.proof: If $F$ and $G$ are faithful and $(G \circ F)(f_1) = (G \circ F)(f_2)$, then $G(F(f_1)) = G(F(f_2))$; faithfulness of $G$ gives $F(f_1) = F(f_2)$; faithfulness of $F$ gives $f_1 = f_2$. $\square$

record.name: Identity functor
record.statement: For any category $\mathcal{C}$, the *identity functor* $\text{Id}_{\mathcal{C}} : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{C}$ defined by $\text{Id}(A) = A$ and $\text{Id}(f) = f$ is a functor. It is the identity for functor composition: $F \circ \text{Id} = F$ and $\text{Id} \circ F = F$.

record.name: Functor composition is associative
record.statement: $(H \circ G) \circ F = H \circ (G \circ F)$ for all composable functors $F, G, H$.
record.proof: On objects and morphisms, both sides evaluate to $H(G(F(-)))$. $\square$

record.name: Natural transformation
record.statement:
Let $F, G : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}$ be functors. A *natural transformation* $\eta : F \Rightarrow G$ is a family of morphisms $\{\eta_A : F(A) \to G(A)\}_{A \in \text{Ob}(\mathcal{C})}$ such that for every morphism $f : A \to B$ in $\mathcal{C}$, the following *naturality square* commutes:

$$
G(f) \circ \eta_A = \eta_B \circ F(f)
$$

Each $\eta_A$ is called a *component* of $\eta$.

record.name: Natural isomorphism
record.statement: A natural transformation $\eta : F \Rightarrow G$ is a *natural isomorphism* if every component $\eta_A$ is an isomorphism in $\mathcal{D}$.

record.name: Vertical composition
record.statement: Given $\eta : F \Rightarrow G$ and $\mu : G \Rightarrow H$ where $F, G, H : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}$, the *vertical composition* $\mu \cdot \eta : F \Rightarrow H$ defined by $(\mu \cdot \eta)_A := \mu_A \circ \eta_A$ is a natural transformation.
record.proof:
For $f : A \to B$:

$$
H(f) \circ (\mu_A \circ \eta_A) = (H(f) \circ \mu_A) \circ \eta_A = (\mu_B \circ G(f)) \circ \eta_A = \mu_B \circ (G(f) \circ \eta_A) = \mu_B \circ (\eta_B \circ F(f)) = (\mu_B \circ \eta_B) \circ F(f)
$$

using naturality of $\mu$ and $\eta$ respectively. $\square$

record.name: Identity natural transformation
record.statement: For any functor $F$, the family $\text{id}^F_A := \text{id}_{F(A)}$ is a natural transformation $F \Rightarrow F$, and it is the identity for vertical composition.

record.name: Functor category
record.statement:
For categories $\mathcal{C}$ and $\mathcal{D}$, the *functor category* $[\mathcal{C}, \mathcal{D}]$ has:
- Objects: functors $\mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}$.
- Morphisms: natural transformations.
- Composition: vertical composition (Proposition 1.15).
- Identity: identity natural transformation (Proposition 1.16).

This is a well-defined category: associativity and unit laws follow from those in $\mathcal{D}$.

record.name: Signature
record.statement:
A *signature* (also called *quiver* or *directed graph*) is a quadruple $\Gamma = (V, E, s, t)$ where:
- $V$ is a set of *vertices*.
- $E$ is a set of *edges*.
- $s, t : E \to V$ assign to each edge its *source* and *target*.

A signature is the data that generates a category: vertices become objects, edges become generating morphisms.

record.name: Signature morphism
record.statement:
A *signature morphism* $\phi : \Gamma \to \Gamma'$ is a pair $(\phi_V, \phi_E)$ where $\phi_V : V \to V'$, $\phi_E : E \to E'$, and:

$$
s'(\phi_E(e)) = \phi_V(s(e)), \quad t'(\phi_E(e)) = \phi_V(t(e))
$$

for all $e \in E$.

record.name: Category of signatures
record.statement:
Signatures and signature morphisms form a category $\textbf{Sig}$:
- Composition: $(\psi \circ \phi)_V = \psi_V \circ \phi_V$ and $(\psi \circ \phi)_E = \psi_E \circ \phi_E$.
- Identity: $(\text{id}_V, \text{id}_E)$.
record.proof: Source/target preservation of the composite follows from that of each factor. Associativity and unit laws are inherited from $\textbf{Set}$. $\square$

record.name: Path
record.statement: Let $\Gamma = (V, E, s, t)$ be a signature. A *path* of length $n$ from $v$ to $w$ is a sequence of edges $(e_1, e_2, \ldots, e_n)$ with $s(e_1) = v$, $t(e_n) = w$, and $t(e_i) = s(e_{i+1})$ for $1 \leq i < n$. The *empty path* $\epsilon_v$ at vertex $v$ has length $0$.

record.name: Free category
record.statement:
The *free category* $\text{Free}(\Gamma)$ on a signature $\Gamma = (V, E, s, t)$ is the category with:
- Objects: $\text{Ob}(\text{Free}(\Gamma)) = V$.
- Morphisms: $\text{Hom}(v, w)$ = set of all paths from $v$ to $w$.
- Composition: path concatenation. Given path $p : v \to w$ and $q : w \to x$, their composite $q \circ p$ is the concatenation of $p$ followed by $q$.
- Identity: empty path $\epsilon_v$ at each vertex $v$.

record.name: Free category is well-defined
record.statement: $\text{Free}(\Gamma)$ is a well-defined category.
record.proof:
1. *Composition closure*: concatenating a path $v \to w$ with a path $w \to x$ yields a path $v \to x$.
2. *Associativity*: list concatenation is associative.
3. *Unit laws*: concatenating the empty list on either side is the identity operation. $\square$

record.name: Universal property of free categories
record.statement: Let $\Gamma$ be a signature and $\mathcal{C}$ a category. For any signature morphism $\phi : \Gamma \to U(\mathcal{C})$ (where $U(\mathcal{C})$ is the underlying signature of $\mathcal{C}$), there exists a unique functor $\hat{\phi} : \text{Free}(\Gamma) \to \mathcal{C}$ such that $\hat{\phi}$ restricted to generators equals $\phi$.
record.proof: Define $\hat{\phi}(v) = \phi_V(v)$ on objects. On a path $(e_1, \ldots, e_n)$, define $\hat{\phi}(e_1, \ldots, e_n) = \phi_E(e_n) \circ \cdots \circ \phi_E(e_1)$, and $\hat{\phi}(\epsilon_v) = \text{id}_{\phi_V(v)}$. Identity and composition preservation follow from the definitions. Uniqueness: any functor agreeing on generators must agree on all paths by induction on path length. $\square$

record.name: Free-forgetful adjunction
record.statement: $\text{Free} : \textbf{Sig} \to \textbf{Cat}$ is a functor. It is left adjoint to the forgetful functor $U : \textbf{Cat} \to \textbf{Sig}$.

record.name: Free functor preserves composition
record.statement:
For signature morphisms $\phi : \Gamma_1 \to \Gamma_2$ and $\psi : \Gamma_2 \to \Gamma_3$:

$$
\text{Free}(\psi \circ \phi) = \text{Free}(\psi) \circ \text{Free}(\phi)
$$
record.proof: Both sides agree on generators by definition. By the universal property, they must be equal. $\square$

record.name: Info record
record.statement: $\text{Info} := \text{String} \to \text{Option}(\text{Value})$. Open key-value structure.

record.name: Astrolabe signature
record.statement:
A triple $\Sigma = (O, M, h)$ where:
- $O : \text{Type } u$, equipped with $\text{info}_O : O \to \text{Info}$
- $M : \text{Type } u$, equipped with $s, t : M \to O$ and $\text{info}_M : M \to \text{Info}$
- $h : O + M \to \text{Hex}_{12}$, injective

Objects and morphisms are first-class citizens sharing identifier space $\text{Hex}_{12}$.

record.name: Metadata space
record.statement: $\mathcal{M}$ is a set of designated keys in Info. $\mathcal{M} \subset \mathcal{M}'$ means $\mathcal{M}'$ has more keys.

record.name: Astrolabe signature morphism
record.statement:
A pair $\phi = (\phi_O, \phi_M)$ mapping objects and morphisms between signatures while preserving source and target:

$$
s(\phi_M(e)) = \phi_O(s(e)), \quad t(\phi_M(e)) = \phi_O(t(e))
$$

Signature morphisms compose: $(\psi \circ \phi)_O = \psi_O \circ \phi_O$, $(\psi \circ \phi)_M = \psi_M \circ \phi_M$.

record.name: signature.json
record.statement: In the reference implementation, $O$ is the "obj" dictionary (key = $h$, value = open info), $M$ is the "mor" dictionary (key = $h$, value includes source, target, and open info). The storage is field-agnostic — it only requires $h$ for obj, and $h + s + t$ for mor. All other keys are written by functors.

record.name: Astrolabe category
record.statement: $\mathcal{A}(\Sigma) := \text{Free}(O, M, s, t)$. The free category generated by signature $\Sigma$.

record.name: Astrolabe category well-definedness
record.statement:
$\mathcal{A}(\Sigma)$ satisfies the four category axioms:
1. Identity: empty path $\epsilon_o$ at each object
2. Composition closure: path concatenation
3. Associativity: list concatenation is associative
4. Unit laws: empty list concatenation

record.name: Representability
record.statement: $\mathcal{A}(\Sigma)$ is completely determined by its generators $(O, M, h)$. Composite morphisms are computed on demand via the universal property of free categories.

record.name: Universal property of astrolabe category
record.statement: For any category $\mathcal{C}$ and quiver morphism $\phi : \Sigma \to \mathcal{C}$, there exists a unique functor $F : \mathcal{A}(\Sigma) \to \mathcal{C}$ with $F \circ \text{generators} = \phi$.

record.name: Equivalence from morphisms
record.statement: Morphisms with info.sort = "equivalent" induce an equivalence relation $\sim$ on objects: symmetric (automatic reverse mor), transitive (composition).

record.name: Quotient well-definedness
record.statement: $\mathcal{A}(\Sigma)/{\sim}$ is a well-defined quotient category. The relation $\sim$ is compatible with morphism structure: given $f : a \to b$, $a \sim a'$, $b \sim b'$, construct $f' := p_b \circ f \circ p_a^{-1}$.

record.name: Projection preserves structure
record.statement: The projection $\pi : \mathcal{A}(\Sigma) \to \mathcal{A}(\Sigma)/{\sim}$ preserves composition and identity.

record.name: Content addressing
record.statement: Two signatures merged. Objects with same content hash $h_c$ get equivalent mor. The induced $\sim$ satisfies reflexivity, symmetry, transitivity, morphism compatibility.

record.name: Astrolabe universe
record.statement: $\mathcal{U} : \text{Type}(u+1)$ such that for any $\mathcal{A}(\Sigma) : \text{Type } u$, there exists a faithful functor $\iota_\Sigma : \mathcal{A}(\Sigma) \hookrightarrow \mathcal{U}$.

record.name: Enrichment closure
record.statement: For $\mathcal{M} \subset \mathcal{M}'$, the diagram commutes: $\iota = \iota' \circ j$ where $j$ is the inclusion.

record.name: Quotient closure
record.statement: For compatible $\sim$, the diagram commutes: $\iota = \iota'' \circ \pi$.

record.name: Content addressing compatibility
record.statement: $h_c(o_1) = h_c(o_2) \implies \iota_{\Sigma_1}(o_1) = \iota_{\Sigma_2}(o_2)$ in $\mathcal{U}$.

record.name: Import functor
record.statement: Any signature morphism $\phi$ preserving source and target extends to a functor $F_\phi : \text{Free}(\mathcal{E}) \to \mathcal{A}(\Sigma)$.

record.name: Import functor preserves composition
record.statement: $F_{\psi \circ \phi} = F_\phi \circ F_\psi$.

record.name: Injective import is faithful
record.statement: If $\phi$ is injective, $F_\phi$ is faithful on generators.

record.name: Natural transformation override
record.statement: Given $R_1, R_2 : \mathcal{A}(\Sigma) \to \mathcal{V}$ and predicate $\varphi$ on info, define $\eta_A := g_A$ if $\varphi(\text{info}(A))$, else $\eta_A := \text{id}$.

record.name: Natural transformation well-definedness
record.statement: $\eta$ is a natural transformation: the override decision depends only on info of each object, not on morphisms, so naturality is automatic.

record.name: Signature category
record.statement: Signature morphisms compose associatively with identity. Signatures and their morphisms form a category $\text{Sig}$.

record.name: Free is a functor
record.statement: $\text{Free} : \text{Sig} \to \text{Cat}$ is a functor: preserves identity ($F_{\text{id}} = \text{id}$) and composition ($F_{\psi \circ \phi} = F_\phi \circ F_\psi$).

record.name: Enrichment functor well-definedness
record.statement: Enrichment functors are well-defined (identity on obj/mor → preserves id and comp).

record.name: Enrichment functor is faithful
record.statement: Enrichment functors are faithful (identity on morphisms → injective).

record.name: Composition of enrichment functors
record.statement: Composition of enrichment functors is an enrichment functor.

record.name: Math domain idempotent
record.statement: Applying math domain defaults twice = applying once.

record.name: Timestamp commutes with math domain
record.statement: Disjoint keys → order doesn't matter.

record.notes: The proof uses the unit laws from the definition of a category.
Set and Grp are concrete instances of the definition of a category.
Initial object is defined within a category.
Uniqueness follows from the definition of initial object.
A functor is a structure-preserving map between categories.
Faithful is a property of a functor.
Full is a property of a functor.
Uses the definition of faithful functor.
Identity functor is a special case of a functor.
Natural transformations are defined between functors.
Natural isomorphism is a special case of natural transformation.
Natural transformations can be composed vertically.
Identity natural transformation is a special case.
Vertical composition gives the composition law for the functor category.
Identity natural transformation gives the identity for the functor category.
The functor category is itself a category.
Signature morphisms are maps between signatures.
Signature morphisms are morphisms in Sig.
Paths are defined on a signature.
Free category is generated by a signature.
Verifies the free category satisfies category axioms.
Universal property characterizes the free category.
The universal property produces a unique functor.
The adjunction follows from the universal property.
Uses the universal property for uniqueness.
Associativity and unit laws inherited from Set.
Astrolabe signature uses Info for metadata.
Astrolabe signature extends the general notion of signature with Info and hex IDs.
Metadata space is a subset of Info keys.
Astrolabe signature morphism specializes the general notion.
Signature morphisms map between astrolabe signatures.
Astrolabe category is the free category on the signature.
Astrolabe category is defined as a free category.
Universal property of the astrolabe category.
Specializes the universal property of free categories.
Equivalence relation defined on objects of astrolabe category.
Quotient is well-defined given the equivalence.
Content addressing is a concrete instance of equivalence.
Each astrolabe category embeds into the universe.
Enrichment closure uses metadata space inclusion.
Enrichment closure is a property of the universe.
Quotient closure is a property of the universe.
Content addressing compatibility follows from quotient closure.
Import functor is induced by a signature morphism.
Uses the definition of faithful functor.
Override is a specific construction of natural transformation.
Free is a functor from Sig to Cat.
Math domain is a specific enrichment functor.
Timestamp and math domain are enrichment functors.
Commutativity involves both math domain and timestamp.
